categories.name: Containers
categories.description: Run, manage, inspect, and debug containers

commands.name: docker run
commands.summary: Create and start a new container from an image

options.description: Interactive with TTY — for shell sessions
Assign a name to the container
Publish port: map host port to container port
Mount a volume or bind-mount a directory
Load environment variables from a file
Automatically remove container when it exits

examples.description: Run nginx in background with port mapping
Interactive shell inside a container
Run with env file and volume mount

commands.name: docker exec
commands.summary: Run a command inside a running container

options.description: Interactive TTY — required for shell sessions
Set environment variable for the exec session
Set working directory inside the container

examples.description: Open a bash shell inside a running container
Run a one-off command
Check environment variables inside a container

commands.name: docker logs
commands.summary: Fetch logs from a container's stdout/stderr

options.description: Follow log output in real time

examples.description: Follow logs for a container
Show logs from the last 30 minutes

commands.name: docker inspect
commands.summary: Return detailed low-level JSON information about a container, image, or network

options.description: Format output using a Go template

examples.description: Get a container's IP address
Get mounted volumes
Check restart policy and status

commands.name: docker stats
commands.summary: Live stream of container resource usage (CPU, memory, network, I/O)

options.description: One-shot snapshot instead of live stream

examples.description: Live stats for all running containers
One-shot snapshot for a specific container

categories.name: Images
categories.description: Build, tag, push, and clean up container images

commands.name: docker build
commands.summary: Build an image from a Dockerfile

options.description: Name and tag the resulting image
Build to a specific multi-stage target

examples.description: Build and tag an image from current directory
Build without cache for a clean build
Multi-platform build

commands.name: docker push / pull
commands.summary: Push or pull images to/from a registry

options.description: Push/pull all tags for the repository

examples.description: Push a tagged image to Docker Hub
Pull a specific version

commands.name: docker image prune
commands.summary: Remove dangling (untagged) or unused images to free disk space

options.description: Remove all unused images (not just dangling)
Filter by label or date (e.g. until=24h)

examples.description: Remove all dangling images
Remove images older than 48h

categories.name: Compose
categories.description: Manage multi-container applications with docker compose

commands.name: docker compose up
commands.summary: Create and start containers defined in docker-compose.yml

options.description: Recreate containers even if config unchanged
Start N replicas of a service

examples.description: Start all services in background, rebuilding images
Start only a specific service

commands.name: docker compose down
commands.summary: Stop and remove containers, networks created by compose up

examples.description: Stop and remove everything including volumes

options.description: Number of lines from the end

examples.description: Follow logs for all services
Follow logs for a specific service

categories.name: Cleanup & Maintenance
categories.description: Free up disk space and manage Docker resources

commands.name: docker system prune
commands.summary: Remove all stopped containers, unused networks, dangling images, and build cache

options.description: Also remove unused images (not just dangling)

examples.description: Full cleanup of all unused Docker resources

commands.note: This is irreversible. Do not use --volumes unless you're sure no important data is in unnamed volumes.

commands.name: docker system df
commands.summary: Show Docker disk usage by images, containers, volumes, and cache

examples.description: Check how much disk Docker is using
